description: Test cases for C++ project file cleanup and organization skill

evals.name: simple-backup-cleanup
evals.prompt: My C++ project has accumulated some backup files in the test directory. I see files like test_main.cc.backup, test_main.cc.backup2, and some .old files. Can you clean these up and organize the project structure?
evals.expected_output: Backup files should be moved to an archive directory, .gitignore should be updated to prevent future backup files, and the project should still build and test successfully.

evals.name: messy-documentation
evals.prompt: The docs folder is a mess. There are like 30 markdown files all mixed together - tdd reports, phase reports, validation reports, some backup files. I can't find anything. Can you organize this properly? Archive the old reports and create an index so I can actually find the documentation I need?
evals.expected_output: Documentation should be organized with archive/tdd_reports/, archive/legacy_docs/, and archive/backups/ subdirectories. A docs/README.md index should be created with clear categorization and navigation. Current essential docs should remain easily accessible.

evals.name: missing-gitignore
evals.prompt: I'm working on a C++ project that doesn't have a .gitignore file. There are build artifacts, backup files, and temporary files cluttering the repository. Can you set up proper file organization and create a comprehensive .gitignore?
evals.expected_output: A comprehensive .gitignore should be created covering build directories, backup files, IDE files, temporary files, logs, and OS-generated files. Existing backup/temporary files should be moved to archive directories. The .gitignore should follow best practices for C++ projects.
